title: ASML Reports Record EUV Orders as AI Chip Demand Surges Past Expectations
excerpt: ASML Holding NV reported record quarterly orders for its extreme ultraviolet lithography machines, driven by surging demand from chipmakers racing to build AI-capable processors. The Dutch company's order book exceeded €9.2 billion.

title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of N2 Node, Targets AI Accelerators
excerpt: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. has commenced volume production on its 2-nanometer process node, with first chips targeting next-generation AI accelerators. TSMC expects N2 to deliver 25% speed improvement over N3E.

title: Nvidia Blackwell B300 GPUs Enter Mass Production at Record Pace
excerpt: Nvidia's next-generation Blackwell B300 GPUs have entered mass production, with the company reporting unprecedented demand from hyperscale cloud providers. Data center revenue is expected to exceed $45B this quarter.

title: AMD MI400 Series Challenges Nvidia with 40% Memory Bandwidth Improvement
excerpt: AMD unveiled its MI400 series accelerators featuring HBM4 memory with 40% more bandwidth than the previous generation, directly targeting Nvidia's dominance in the AI training market.

title: Intel 18A Process Node Achieves Yield Milestone, Secures Major Foundry Client
excerpt: Intel's 18A manufacturing process has reached a critical yield milestone, and the company has secured a significant foundry customer for high-volume production starting Q1 2026. Shares jumped 8% on the news.

title: SK Hynix HBM4 Production Sold Out Through 2026 as AI Demand Intensifies
excerpt: SK Hynix announced that its HBM4 high-bandwidth memory production is fully allocated through 2026, with Nvidia and AMD as primary customers. The company is investing $12B to expand capacity.

title: US Tightens China Chip Export Controls, Restricts Advanced EUV Equipment
excerpt: The Biden administration announced expanded export restrictions on semiconductor equipment to China, including stricter controls on EUV lithography components. ASML and Applied Materials face new compliance requirements.

title: Meta Plans $65B AI Infrastructure Spend in 2025, Orders Custom Chips
excerpt: Meta Platforms revealed plans to spend $65 billion on AI infrastructure in 2025, including custom-designed training chips manufactured by TSMC and massive new data center campuses across the US.

title: Google DeepMind Unveils Gemini Ultra 2.0 with Breakthrough Reasoning
excerpt: Google DeepMind released Gemini Ultra 2.0, demonstrating significant advances in multi-step reasoning and code generation. The model outperforms GPT-5 on several key benchmarks.

title: Applied Materials Reports Strong Etch Equipment Demand from Leading-Edge Fabs
excerpt: Applied Materials reported better-than-expected Q3 results, with etch and deposition equipment sales surging 28% year-over-year as chipmakers expand leading-edge capacity for AI workloads.

title: Broadcom's AI Networking Revenue Triples as Custom Silicon Business Booms
excerpt: Broadcom reported AI-related revenue tripling year-over-year, driven by custom AI accelerator designs for hyperscale clients and surging demand for its networking silicon used in GPU clusters.

title: SOX Index Hits All-Time High as Semiconductor Stocks Rally on AI Optimism
excerpt: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) surged to a record high, gaining 3.2% as investors bet on sustained AI infrastructure spending. All major chip stocks posted gains.

title: Amazon AWS Launches Custom Trainium3 Chips, Cuts AI Training Costs 50%
excerpt: Amazon Web Services unveiled its third-generation Trainium AI training chips, claiming 50% lower training costs compared to GPU-based alternatives. The chips are manufactured on TSMC's N3E process.

title: Lam Research Wins Major Orders for Gate-All-Around Etch Tools
excerpt: Lam Research secured significant new orders for its gate-all-around transistor etch equipment as Samsung and Intel ramp their next-generation chip manufacturing processes.

title: China's SMIC Reportedly Achieves 5nm-Class Production Without EUV
excerpt: China's largest chipmaker SMIC has reportedly achieved stable production of 5nm-class chips using multi-patterning DUV lithography, raising questions about the effectiveness of export controls.

title: Microsoft Azure Expands AI Cloud Capacity with $50B Data Center Investment
excerpt: Microsoft announced a $50 billion investment in new data center capacity focused on AI workloads, including facilities in the US, Europe, and Asia. The investment will deploy thousands of Nvidia and AMD GPUs.

title: KLA Corporation Sees Record Demand for AI Chip Inspection Tools
excerpt: KLA Corporation reported record orders for its advanced inspection and metrology tools, driven by the complexity of manufacturing AI chips at leading-edge nodes. Revenue guidance was raised 15%.

title: OpenAI and Anthropic Drive Enterprise AI Adoption to Record Levels
excerpt: Enterprise adoption of large language models has reached record levels, with OpenAI and Anthropic each reporting over $5B in annualized revenue. The surge is driving unprecedented demand for AI compute infrastructure.

title: Japan Announces $15B Semiconductor Subsidy Package to Attract Fab Investment
excerpt: Japan announced an expanded $15 billion subsidy package to attract semiconductor manufacturing investment, targeting TSMC's planned third fab and new facilities from Samsung and Micron.

title: Synopsys and Cadence Report Surge in AI-Driven EDA Tool Demand
excerpt: Synopsys and Cadence Design Systems both reported accelerating demand for AI-enhanced EDA tools, as chip designers increasingly rely on machine learning to optimize complex processor designs.

title: Analysts Warn of AI Capex Bubble as Hyperscaler Spending Exceeds Returns
excerpt: Several prominent Wall Street analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of AI infrastructure spending, noting that combined hyperscaler capex now exceeds $300B annually with unclear return timelines.

title: Arm Holdings Sees Royalty Revenue Spike from AI Smartphone Chip Designs
excerpt: Arm Holdings reported a 35% jump in royalty revenue as smartphone makers adopted its latest AI-optimized CPU architectures. The company raised its full-year guidance citing strong design win momentum.

title: Entegris Expands Advanced Materials Capacity for EUV Lithography
excerpt: Entegris announced a $1.2 billion expansion of its specialty materials manufacturing to support growing demand for EUV photoresists and advanced filtration systems used in sub-3nm chip production.

title: EU CHIPS Act Funding Accelerates with €8B Allocated to New Fabs
excerpt: The European Commission approved €8 billion in subsidies under the EU CHIPS Act, funding new semiconductor facilities from Intel, TSMC, and GlobalFoundries across Germany, France, and Ireland.

title: Micron Technology Raises HBM Revenue Forecast Amid AI Memory Shortage
excerpt: Micron raised its HBM revenue guidance by 30%, citing tight supply conditions and premium pricing for high-bandwidth memory used in AI accelerators. HBM now represents 25% of total DRAM revenue.
